seo.title: Building Code Generation with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with Semantic Kernel enables powerful LLM-driven development workflows, from initial code synthesis to iterative refinement through sandbox execution and testing. However, the high-risk nature of generating and executing code introduces severe risks like credential leakage, unsafe dependencies, and syntax errors that can cascade through plugin chains. Vex integration provides essential guardrails to verify code safety, execution integrity, and data isolation before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Semantic Kernel code generation architecture starts with a Planner that orchestrates plugins for requirements analysis, code synthesis, sandbox execution, and test validation. The agent iteratively refines code through memory recall of previous iterations while plugins handle specific tasks like syntax checking, security scanning, and unit test generation. Function calling flows through the kernel's invocation pipeline, with results feeding back into the planner for plan adjustment.

Key components include CodeGeneratorPlugin (synthesizes code), SandboxExecutorPlugin (runs code safely), TestValidatorPlugin (generates/runs tests), and MemoryStore (tracks iteration history). The high-risk profile demands verification at each stage to prevent malicious code injection or execution failures.

Vex integrates as a result filter, automatically scanning outputs for safety violations before they propagate through the kernel.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Code generator produces invalid syntax вҶ’ sandbox fails вҶ’ test validator receives broken code вҶ’ planner loops indefinitely
risks.mitigation: Vex result filtering catches syntax errors immediately after code generation

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Planner sequences sandbox execution before code validation, causing premature dangerous execution
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies execution order and preconditions in plan outputs

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Agent recalls non-existent successful test runs, skipping validation on contaminated code
risks.mitigation: Vex validates memory retrievals against execution history

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Test results from previous iteration leak API keys into next code generation cycle
risks.mitigation: Vex scans all plugin outputs for credential leakage patterns

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Kernel Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Semantic Kernel with core code generation plugins and planner
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

kernel = Kernel()

# Add LLM service
kernel.add_chat_service(
    "gpt-4",
    OpenAIChatCompletion(
        deployment_name="gpt-4",
        ai_model_id="gpt-4",
    ),
)

# Add code generation plugin
code_plugin = kernel.import_plugin_from_prompt_directory("plugins/code_gen")

# Create planner
planner = kernel.add_planner(planners.SequentialPlanner())

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Plugin
implementation.description: Execute generated code in isolated Docker sandbox with timeout protection
implementation.code:
import docker

class SandboxExecutor:
    def execute_code(self, code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
        client = docker.from_env()
        
        # Create temporary container with Python
        container = client.containers.run(
            "python:3.11-slim",
            command=f"timeout {timeout} python -c '{code}'",
            detach=True,
            remove=True,
            mem_limit="256m",
            network_disabled=True
        )
        
        result = container.wait()
        logs = container.logs().decode()
        
        return {
            "exit_code": result["StatusCode"],
            "output": logs,
            "success": result["StatusCode"] == 0
        }

vexIntegration.title: Python: Vex Kernel Result Filter
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as post-invocation filter to scan all plugin outputs for code safety
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from semantic_kernel import KernelFunction

# Initialize Vex
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-your-key")

# Create Vex filter function
def vex_code_safety_filter(
    function_result: str, 
    function_name: str
) -> str:
    
    # High-risk verifications for code generation
    checks = [
        "code_safety",
        "syntax_validity",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "execution_sandboxing",
        "dependency_safety"
    ]
    
    verdict = vex.verify({
        "input": function_result,
        "context": {"plugin": function_name},
        "policy": "code-generation-high-risk",
        "checks": checks
    })
    
    if not verdict.approved:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked unsafe code: {verdict.reason}")
    
    return function_result

# Register filter on kernel
kernel.register_function_result_filter(vex_code_safety_filter)

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript: Vex Async Result Handler
vexIntegration.description: Integrate Vex verification in async kernel invocation pipeline

vexIntegration.title: Python: Planner Plan Validation
vexIntegration.description: Verify execution plans contain safe sequencing before kernel execution
vexIntegration.code:
def validate_execution_plan(plan_steps: list) -> bool:
    vex_plan_check = vex.verify({
        "input": plan_steps,
        "context": {"type": "execution_plan"},
        "policy": "semantic-kernel-planner",
        "checks": [
            "safe_execution_order",
            "sandbox_before_execution",
            "validation_before_production"
        ]
    })
    
    return vex_plan_check.approved

# Use in planner workflow
plan = planner.create_plan(user_input)
if not validate_execution_plan(plan):
    plan = planner.create_plan(f"SAFER version: {user_input}")

result = await kernel.run_async(plan)

cta.heading: Secure Your Semantic Kernel Code Generation
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and add result filtering to eliminate code generation risks today.
